summary: Iter12 (Editable Testimonials admin CRUD + Subscription expiry-reminder cron) end-to-end tested. Backend: 10/10 iter12 tests pass. Frontend E2E covered all critical flows: public landing /testimonials-section renders 3 visible default cards; admin /admin/testimonials route loads via session-token cookie; sidebar nav link present (data-testid='admin-nav-testimonials'); Add → Edit (name/quote/4-star) → Save persists across reload; Reset restores defaults. Startup log line confirmed: '[STARTUP] subscription expiry reminder scheduler launched · interval=3600s · lead_days=[3, 1, 0]'. Stub-mode flags verified in cron response (email_stub=true, sms_stub=true). Idempotency confirmed: 3 dedupe rows in db.expiry_reminders_sent for T+3/T+1/T+0 seeded subs, second run produced skipped>=3 with no new rows.

minor.issue: Rating clamp uses `int(t.rating or 5)` which treats explicit 0 as falsy and silently substitutes default 5 instead of clamping to min 1. Not user-facing today (UI uses 1-5 stars only) but a brittle pattern. Suggest `int(t.rating) if t.rating is not None else 5` then clamp.

action_items: (MINOR / non-blocking) Tighten rating coercion in PUT /api/admin/testimonials to honor explicit 0 (clamp to 1) instead of falling back to default 5 due to Python falsy-0. One-liner change in /app/backend/server.py L2342.
critical_code_review_comments: Testimonials CRUD endpoints are correctly admin-gated (401 no-auth, 403 non-admin role) and public GET correctly filters by `visible:true`. ✅
expiry_reminder_loop schedules are launched on startup; idempotency via compound key (sub_id, days_left, sent_date) verified end-to-end. ✅
_load_testimonials() returns sorted-by-order results — order field truncation/typing is via `int(t.order if t.order is not None else i)`, robust. ✅
Stub-mode wiring is correct: cron response now includes both `email_stub` and `sms_stub` so a tester can verify nothing is going out over the wire when keys are blank.
Minor — rating coercion `int(t.rating or 5)` treats integer 0 as falsy. Replace with explicit None check.

Frontend AdminTestimonials.jsx — image upload reads File via FileReader → data URL and stores in image_url; backend caps at 2 MB. UX note: no client-side size warning before the request fails. Consider showing a toast if file > 1.5 MB before upload.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — public testimonials section, admin CRUD page load, add/edit/save persistence, reset, sidebar nav all verified

test_credentials: Mongo-injected admin session_token (TEST_st_*) for /admin/* UI testing; admin@efoodcare.com is in ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist.
seed_data_creation: Per-test transient TEST_-prefixed admin/subscriber users + sessions for backend tests. test_run_with_seeded_subs_and_idempotent inserts 3 active subscriptions with end_date offsets {3,1,0} and removes them + their dedupe rows in teardown. UI test seeded a TEST_admin_* + TEST_st_* pair into Mongo (cleaned up post-run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run iter12 backend: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://dining-pass-scan.preview.emergentagent.com MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database pytest backend/tests/test_iter12.py -v`. For UI testing /admin/testimonials, seed (user with role='admin' + matching session in db.user_sessions) and set cookie name 'session_token' (Secure, SameSite=None) on dining-pass-scan.preview.emergentagent.com, then visit /admin/testimonials. Public landing /testimonials-section is anonymous. Email/SMS are in stub mode (RESEND_API_KEY blank, MSG91_AUTH_KEY blank), so `/admin/cron/run-expiry-reminders` returns email_stub:true, sms_stub:true and counters; the dedupe collection is db.expiry_reminders_sent.
